reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle is simple (7 × 9 = 63), so I’m confident Q1-A is correct. In all previous games, everyone’s puzzles have also been basic arithmetic, and trusting each player’s own capsule set (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D) has always yielded a correct password. Verifying would cost coins and still leave the risk of a no-volunteer round and random elimination. By volunteering immediately with everyone’s own capsules, I avoid that random-death risk, spend no coins, and the chance another player mis-solved their puzzle seems extremely low.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D

All questions have been simple arithmetic with no past mistakes, and there’s only one capsule per question, so there are no conflicts. Verifying would burn coins without much extra safety and passing risks a random death if others also wait. Volunteering now with the only available capsules gives me the best chance to survive with all 4 coins.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D

There’s only one capsule per question again, and I’m fully confident my own answer to 99 ÷ 9 is correct. Verifying would just waste coins, and passing keeps the risk that no one volunteers and a random player dies. Volunteering now with the single capsule for each question gives a very high chance of success and avoids the random-death risk.
